role_title: Senior Manager, Claims Vendor Strategy & Operations

seniority.level: Senior Manager / Lead IC or People Manager
level_signals: 5+ years in strategy, vendor management, or claims operations
Comp range $110K-$135K base consistent with Senior Manager at a Series-D insurtech

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years in strategy, vendor management, or claims operations, ideally in a tech-forward or high growth environment
must_haves.qualifier: ideally in a tech-forward or high growth environment

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3+ years in a leadership role, with a track record of managing and scaling complex partner relationships
Advanced data analysis skills a MUST, including SQL or BI tools, and the ability to turn large, imperfect datasets into clear financial decisions
Demonstrated experience leading cross functional initiatives from kickoff to measurable results
Executive level communication skills, you can influence a partner CEO and an internal Product lead in the same afternoon

At Lemonade we welcome candidates who are enthusiastic about learning and adapting to the exciting world of technology and AI, as a commitment to ongoing growth in this field is a fundamental part of our culture
Ability to work in a remote environment

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Insurtech or startup experience is a strong plus

explicitly_excluded: Applicants from Alaska, California, Colorado, Montana, Hawaii, New Mexico, and Puerto Rico

scope.surface: Property Claims – Third Party Administrators and operational vendors
core_responsibilities: Own the end-to-end strategy for TPAs and operational vendors from selection and onboarding through ongoing optimization and performance management
Set and enforce rigorous KPIs and SLAs, holding external partners to the same standards of speed, care, and accuracy we hold ourselves to
Partner with Product and Engineering to integrate vendors into our proprietary AI tools, pushing legacy partners toward a faster, tech-first way of working
Build executive ready business cases using financial modeling to influence Loss Adjustment Expense and vendor budgeting decisions
Lead regular quality audits and performance reviews, translating data into concrete plans that improve vendor output and cost efficiency
Surface performance insights with Analytics and work directly with vendors to embed those insights into their day-to-day workflows

green_flags: Compensation range clearly disclosed ($110K-$135K base) plus equity
Fully remote role (LI-Remote tag in JD body)
Clear ownership scope: end-to-end TPA and vendor strategy within Property Claims
AI-first culture with explicit investment in tooling integration
Role has meaningful executive visibility (building business cases, influencing partner CEOs)
red_flags: Hard state exclusion list (AK, CA, CO, MT, HI, NM, Puerto Rico) significantly limits eligible remote candidates
No visa sponsorship available — explicitly stated
Compensation range ($110K-$135K) may be below market for a Senior Manager with 5+ years experience at a high-growth tech company, especially given the exclusion of higher-cost states
College Degree listed as a hard requirement with no 'equivalent experience' qualifier

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title, years required, and comp range)
seniority.manages_people (inferred from 'leadership role' and 'managing and scaling complex partner relationships')
scope.business_model (inferred from Lemonade being a B2C insurtech)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly listed in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from JD language 'salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location')
ambiguities: The JD includes '||#LI-Remote||' tag suggesting fully remote, and the body says 'Ability to work in a remote environment', but salary varies by location — could imply some location restrictions beyond the excluded states list. Treated as remote-us here.
College Degree requirement has no 'or equivalent experience' clause, unlike many JDs — treated as hard requirement, though some recruiters may apply discretion.
The 5+ years requirement includes 'ideally in a tech-forward or high growth environment' which softens it slightly, but the unconditional bullet placement suggests hard severity.
